narrative.headline: 2020 Honda Odyssey: electronics and driver-assist lead the complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 402 complaints with NHTSA. The two biggest areas are nearly tied: the electrical system at 150 reports (37%) and forward collision avoidance at 148 reports (37%), followed by lane departure at 106 (26%). No deaths are reported, and crashes and fires are rare across every area. Most reports describe electronics and driver-assist features misbehaving rather than serious safety failures.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 150 reports (37%), with 1 injury and no crashes or fires. Owners describe screens, audio, and electronics glitching or rebooting while driving.

issues.sample: MAJOR SAFETY CONCERN (BRAKE FAIL DURING DRIVING) WITH NEW 2020 HONDA ODYSSEY WITH 2618 MILES DRIVEN. BRAKE MADE LOUD SQUEAK NOISE RIGHT AFTER PURCHASE AND TOOK IT TO LOCAL HONDA DEALERSHIP WHERE CAR WAS PURCHASED 4 TIMES. HONDA DEALERSHIP RESURFACED ROTOR 2 DIFFERENT TIMES. ABOUT
issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: Nearly tied for first: 148 reports (37%), linked to 1 crash and 1 injury. Worth testing the collision and braking assist systems on a drive.

issues.sample: The following errors suddenly appeared one-at-a-time, causing all the advanced driver assistance capabilities to stop working, including regular cruise control:  * ACC - Adaptive Cruise Control Problem * LKAS - Lane Keeping Assist Problem * Collision Mitigation System Problem * R
issues.title: Lane Departure
issues.blurb: Third most reported: 106 reports (26%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Owners describe lane-keeping and other driver-assist features throwing errors and shutting off.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 57 reports (14%), including 1 fire, 1 crash, and 1 injury. A smaller area, but the one fire makes it worth a careful check.

issues.title: Structure
issues.blurb: The fewest reports (28, 7%) but the most injuries of any area (3), with no crashes or fires. Several owners describe the rear sliding doors opening on their own.
